description:
You have just joined a small web–development team that is prototyping a user-login micro-service.  
They already agreed on an extremely simple authentication model that stores the users in an on-disk SQLite
database, and they want a tiny proof-of-concept created entirely from the command line.

Your goal is to create that proof-of-concept database, populate it with three sample users,
and save the result of a specific query to a text file so that the automated tests can
check your work.

Desired final state of the system
1. Directory hierarchy  
   • /home/user/dev_data/ must exist (create it if it does not).  
   • All artefacts that you create in this task must live inside that directory.

2. SQLite database  
   • File name: /home/user/dev_data/site_auth.db  
   • It must contain exactly one table, named users, with **precisely** the following definition  
     ```
     CREATE TABLE users(
       id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
       username TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
       password TEXT NOT NULL
     );
     ```  
     – The column order, names, types and constraints must match this definition exactly.  
     – No other tables, views, triggers or indices may exist in the database.

3. Sample data inside table users  
   Insert **exactly three** rows, in this order:

           id | username | password
           1  | alice    | alice_pass
           2  | bob      | bob_secret
           3  | charlie  | charlie_pwd

   The numeric IDs must be the explicit integer values 1, 2 and 3 (i.e. do not rely on AUTOINCREMENT).

4. Query result log  
   • After the table has been populated, run the query

         SELECT id,username FROM users ORDER BY id;

   • Save **only** the output of this query (no prompt lines, no explanatory text) to the file  
     /home/user/dev_data/query_result.log

   • The required output format is:
       – SQLite list mode with a vertical-bar (“|”) separator.  
       – No header line.  
       – One row per line.  
       – A single trailing newline after the last row.  

     Therefore the exact contents of /home/user/dev_data/query_result.log must be **exactly**

     ```
     1|alice
     2|bob
     3|charlie
     ```

     followed immediately by a single newline character (i.e. the file ends right after the newline following “charlie”).

What you must do (high-level)
a. Create the directory if necessary.  
b. Use the command-line `sqlite3` tool to create the database file and define the table.  
c. Insert the three rows.  
d. Produce the query output in the required format and redirect/pipe it into the log file.  
e. Optionally verify your own work by re-opening the database and inspecting its contents (this will not be checked by the tests, but it can help you spot mistakes).

Important constraints & hints
• You do NOT need root privileges; everything happens in your own home directory.  
• The automated test script will:
  1. Confirm that /home/user/dev_data/site_auth.db exists and is a valid SQLite database.  
  2. Check the exact schema of the users table.  
  3. Confirm that the table contains exactly the three rows listed above and no more.  
  4. Inspect /home/user/dev_data/query_result.log and assert that its byte-for-byte contents match the specification.  
• So be careful about:
  – Column order and names.  
  – Typos in usernames or passwords.  
  – Extra whitespace or blank lines in the log file.  
• Keep your terminal commands concise; you only need a handful (creating the table, inserting rows, querying, and redirecting output).

When finished, you should be able to run the following to view the final result manually:

    sqlite3 /home/user/dev_data/site_auth.db 'SELECT id,username FROM users ORDER BY id;'

and see the three-line, bar-separated output shown above.
truth:
Directory that must exist before agent starts (will be created by test harness, but agent must handle if it is missing):
  /home/user/dev_data/

Files that must exist after agent finishes:
1. /home/user/dev_data/site_auth.db  
   – SQLite database, file header begins with "SQLite format 3".  
   – Exactly one table named "users".  
     PRAGMA table_info(users) must return  
       cid|name|type|notnull|dflt_value|pk  
       0|id|INTEGER|0|<null>|1  
       1|username|TEXT|1|<null>|0  
       2|password|TEXT|1|<null>|0  
   – SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY id; must return exactly  
       1|alice|alice_pass  
       2|bob|bob_secret  
       3|charlie|charlie_pwd  

2. /home/user/dev_data/query_result.log  
   – Must contain exactly the 33 bytes (including newlines)  
     1|alice\n2|bob\n3|charlie\n  
     (where \n denotes Unix LF 0x0a).  
   – File size must therefore be 33 bytes.

No other files should be created inside /home/user/dev_data/.  
Permissions: files should be user-writable (0664 or stricter).

Automated tests will open the database using the sqlite3 CLI and run:
  .mode list
  .separator |
  SELECT id,username FROM users ORDER BY id;
and compare to the expected output.
